examples: Telomerase a eukaryotic ribonucleoprotein complex contains both essential rna and a protein reverse transcriptase subunit.
Telomerase is a ribonucleoprotein enzyme maintaining the length of the telomeres by adding G-rich repeats to the end of the eukaryotic chromosomes.
Telomerase is a ribonucleoprotein enzyme, which is positively expressed in most malignant tumor cells, while negatively expressed in normal somatic ones.
